briefdescription.para: This function will start sending a video stream over the main link.

detaileddescription.para:
The settings to be used are as follows:
8 bits per color.
Video timing and screen resolution used:
The connected monitor's preferred timing is used to determine the video resolution (and associated timings) for the stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

simplesect.para: The Dptx_StreamSrc* are intentionally left for the user to implement since configuration of the stream source is application-specific.
The Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) is read in order to obtain the video resolution and timings. If this read fails, a resolution of 640x480 is used at a refresh rate of 60Hz.

briefdescription.para: This function will configure and establish a link with the receiver device, afterwards, a video stream will start to be sent over the main link.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the number of lanes to use over the main link.
is the link rate to use over the main link.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if main link was successfully established.

briefdescription.para: This function will setup and initialize the DisplayPort TX core.

detaileddescription.para: The core's configuration parameters will be retrieved based on the configuration to the DisplayPort TX core instance with the specified device ID.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the unique device ID of the DisplayPort TX core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the device configuration was found and obtained and if the main link was successfully established.

briefdescription.para: This function will configure and establish a link with the receiver device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS the if main link was successfully established.

briefdescription.para: Contains a design example using the XDp driver (operating in TX mode).

detaileddescription.para: It performs a self test on the DisplayPort TX core by training the main link at the maximum common capabilities between the TX and RX and checking the lane status.

simplesect.para: The DisplayPort TX core does not work alone - video/audio sources need to be set up in the system correctly, as well as setting up the output path (for example, configuring the hardware system with the DisplayPort TX core output to an FMC card with DisplayPort output capabilities. Some platform initialization will need to happen prior to calling XDp driver functions. See XAPP1178 as a reference.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial creation. 5.1 ms 01/23/17 Added xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028.
